sections.title: 2023 AMENDMENTS
sections.text: Following decisions taken at the Forty-sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2023, the food additives provisions were amended in this standard and have been included in the General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) (CXS 192-1995)1 in line with the process of alignment of all food additive provisions with the GSFA.
sections.retrieval_text:

level=1
Following decisions taken at the Forty-sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2023, the food additives provisions were amended in this standard and have been included in the General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) (CXS 192-1995)1 in line with the process of alignment of all food additive provisions with the GSFA.

page_spans.text: Following decisions taken at the Forty-sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2023, the food additives provisions were amended in this standard and have been included in the General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) (CXS 192-1995)1 in line with the process of alignment of all food additive provisions with the GSFA.

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to mango chutney, as defined in Section  2 below and offered for direct consumption, including for catering purposes or for repacking, if required. It does not apply to the product when indicated as being intended for further processing.
sections.retrieval_text:

number=1
level=1

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.retrieval_text:
DESCRIPTION
number=2
level=1

sections.title: Product definition
sections.text:
Mango chutney is the product:  
a) prepared from substantially sound fruits  both ripe and unripe  fresh and/or preserved mango, having reached appropriate maturity for processing . None of their essential  characteristic elements are removed from them . They undergo operations such as sorting, trimming, washing, peeling, cutting, and otherwise treated to remove any blemishes, bruises, toppings, tailings, cores, pits (stone) etc.; 
b) packed with optional ingredients as indicated in Section 3.1.2; and 
c) processed in an appropriate manner, before or after being hermetically sealed in a container or flexible container, so as to prevent spoilage and to ensure product stability in normal storage conditions at ambient temperature.

page_spans.text:
Mango chutney is the product:  
a) prepared from substantially sound fruits  both ripe and unripe  fresh and/or preserved mango, having reached appropriate maturity for processing . None of their essential  characteristic elements are removed from them . They undergo operations such as sorting, trimming, washing, peeling, cutting, and otherwise treated to remove any blemishes, bruises, toppings, tailings, cores, pits (stone) etc.; 
b) packed with optional ingredients as indicated in Section 3.1.2; and 
c) processed in an appropriate manner, before or after being hermetically sealed in a container or flexible container, so as to prevent spoilage and to ensure product stability in normal storage conditions at ambient temperature.

sections.title: Other styles
sections.text:
Any other presentation of the product should be permitted provided that the product:  
a) is sufficiently distinctive from other forms of presentation laid down in the standard; 
b) meets all other requirements of the standard, as applicable; and 
c) is adequately described on the label to avoid confusing or misleading the consumer.

sections.title: Classification of “defectives”
sections.text: A container that fails to meet one or more of the applicable quality requirements, as set out in Section 3.3 and minimum content of mango fruit, as set out in Section 3.2, should be considered as a “defective”.

sections.title: Lot acceptance
sections.text: A lot should be considered as meeting the applicable quality requirements for minimum fruit content referred to in Section 3. 2, and the quality criteria in Section 3.3, when the number of “defectives” does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of an appropriate sampling plan with an acceptable quality level (AQL) of 6.5.

sections.title: FOOD ADDITIVES
sections.text: Acidity regulators and preservatives used in accordance with Table 1 and Table 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives  (CXS 192-1995)1 in food category 04.1.2.6 (Fruit -based spreads (e.g.  chutney) excluding products of food category 04.1.2.5) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this standard and only certain acidity regulators in Table 3 are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this standard.

sections.title: Colour, flavour, odour and texture
sections.text: Mango chutney shall have normal colour, flavour and odour of mango chutney corresponding to the type of fruits and added optional ingredients used and shall possess texture characteristic of the product.

sections.title: Definition of defects
sections.text: Extraneous vegetable material means any vegetable part (such as, but not limited to, a leaf or portion thereof, or a stem) that does not pose any hazard to human health but affects the overall appearance of the final product. Fibrous matter means visible strands of fibre (from fruits and/or other ingredient) of more than 1 cm in length.

sections.title: Defects and allowances
sections.text: The product shall be reasonably free from defects such as seed or particles thereof, peels, grit or any other extraneous vegetable material. The product shall also be reasonably free from discolouration due to oxidation, objectionable, and metallic or off-flavour or foreign odour and fibrous matter.

sections.text: It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Canned Fruit and Vegetable Products  (CXC 2-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Low-Acid and Acidified Low-Acid Canned Foods (CXC 23-1979), and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice.

sections.title: Fill of container
sections.retrieval_text:
WEIGHTS AND MEASURES > FILL OF CONTAINER
number=7.1
level=2

sections.title: Minimum fill
sections.text: a) The container should be well filled with the product which should occupy not less than 90  percent minus any necessary head space according to good manufacturing practices ) of the water capacity of the container . The water capacity of the container is the volume of distilled water at 20  oC which the  sealed container will hold when completely filled . b) Flexible containers should be filled as full as commercially practicable .

sentences.original:
Any other presentation of the product should be permitted provided that the product:  
a) is sufficiently distinctive from other forms of presentation laid down in the standard; 
b) meets all other requirements of the standard, as applicable; and 
c) is adequately described on the label to avoid confusing or misleading the consumer.

Any other presentation of the product should be permitted provided that the product:  
a) is sufficiently distinctive from other forms of presentation laid down in the standard; 
b) meets all other requirements of the standard, as applicable; and 
c) is adequately described on the label to avoid confusing or misleading the consumer.
classification.pattern: [General permission with conditions a-c enumerated]

components.permission_condition: Any other presentation of the product should be permitted
conditions: distinctiveness from other forms of presentation
adequately labeled to avoid consumer confusion

classification.justification: Permission modality qualified by three explicit conditions presented in an enumerated structure.

sentences.original: A container that fails to meet one or more of the applicable quality requirements, as set out in Section 3.3 and minimum content of mango fruit, as set out in Section 3.2, should be considered as a “defective”.

classification.justification: Expresses a condition under which a container is classified as defective with normative implication.

sentences.original: A lot should be considered as meeting the applicable quality requirements for minimum fruit content referred to in Section 3. 2, and the quality criteria in Section 3.3, when the number of “defectives” does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of an appropriate sampling plan with an acceptable quality level (AQL) of 6.5.

classification.sentence: A lot should be considered as meeting the applicable quality requirements for minimum fruit content referred to in Section 3. 2, and the quality criteria in Section 3.3, when the number of “defectives” does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of an appropriate sampling plan with an acceptable quality level (AQL) of 6.5.

classification.justification: Defines acceptance condition for lots using normatively phrased conditional construction.

sentences.original: Acidity regulators and preservatives used in accordance with Table 1 and Table 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives  (CXS 192-1995)1 in food category 04.1.2.6 (Fruit -based spreads (e.g.  chutney) excluding products of food category 04.1.2.5) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this standard and only certain acidity regulators in Table 3 are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this standard.

classification.sentence: In addition, the following specific provisions apply:
classification.pattern: Introductory phrase + declarative statement indicating applicability of provisions
classification.justification: The sentence imposes an obligation by stating that certain provisions apply. Its structure is an introductory phrase followed by a declarative statement. Since no known templates exist and the sentence does not fit typical modal verbs or conditional structures, it is classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and imposition communicative function.
notes: The sentence is an introductory clause serving to announce subsequent obligations, without explicit modal verbs.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR MANGO CHUTNEY
pages.summary: Página inicial do documento com título principal e código padrão no cabeçalho, sem seções ou texto estruturado presentes na página.

A página apresenta dois títulos principais (nível 1) em caixa alta, sem numeração, seguidos por seus respectivos textos explicativos. O cabeçalho da página está presente e identificado. Não há indicação de continuidade com páginas anteriores ou próximas.

Página contém o cabeçalho 'CXS 160-1987  3'. Dois títulos principais nível 1 numerados (1. SCOPE e 2. DESCRIPTION). Segundo título possui subtítulos de níveis 2 e 3 identificados pela numeração (2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.3.1, 2.3.2). O texto é associado corretamente aos seus respectivos nós. O título 2. DESCRIPTION tem filhos e por isso seu próprio texto direto é vazio. A seção 2. ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS começa na sequência, mas pela análise da página atual não aparece, indicando continuidade para a próxima página no 2. DESCRIPTION.

A página apresenta a continuação da seção 3.3 Quality criteria com subtítulos numerados 3.3.1 a 3.3.4 e seus respectivos textos. Seguem-se seções nível 1 numeradas 3.4, 3.5, 4, 5, 6 e 7, algumas com subtítulos nível 2 e 3. Algumas subseções (5.1, 5.2, 6.1, 6.2) não exibem títulos textuais, apenas números de seção. As seções são claramente identificadas por numeração. A seção 3.3 está marcada como continuidade da página anterior, e a seção 7 continua na próxima página.

Nesta página, predominam seções com nível hierárquico 1 e 2 numeradas conforme padrão. A seção 7.1 está presente indiretamente pelos subtítulos 7.1.2 e 7.1.3, sem seu título expresso na página. A seção 8 e seus subtítulos 8.1 e 8.2, além da seção 9, estão claramente indicados com seus textos correspondentes. Não há indicação de continuidade de seções com páginas anteriores ou seguintes nesta página.
